sanskrit_devanagari:
बीजं मां सर्वभूतानां विद्धि पार्थ सनातनम् ।
बुद्धिर्बुद्धिमतामस्मि तेजस्तेजस्विनामहम् ॥ १० ॥
sanskrit_iast:
bījaṁ māṁ sarva-bhūtānāṁ viddhi pārtha sanātanam |
buddhir buddhimatām asmi tejas tejasvinām aham || 10 ||

sanskrit_sources.agreement: exact (raw HTML scrape; akṣara sequence identical).
exact (academic edition; text body identical; punctuation/danda rendering differs).
exact (academic critical edition; cross-reference Bhg_07.010 = MBh_06,029.010). Body text identical.

translations.verbatim_quote: O son of Pṛthā, know that I am the original seed of all existences, the intelligence of the intelligent, and the prowess of all powerful men.
O Arjun, know that I am the eternal seed of all beings. I am the intellect of the intelligent, and the splendor of the glorious.

commentaries.verbatim_excerpt: Bījam means seed; Kṛṣṇa is the seed of everything. There are various living entities, movable and inert. Birds, beasts, men and many other living creatures are moving living entities; trees and plants are inert.

commentaries.translator: Swami Gambhirananda (Advaita Ashrama edition of Sankara-bhasya, as reproduced on bhagavad-gita.us)
commentaries.verbatim_excerpt: 7.10 O Pārtha, viddhi, know, mām, Me; to be the sanātanam, eternal; bījam, seed, the source of growth; sarva-bhūtānām, of all beings. Besides, I am the buddhiḥ, intellect, the power of discrimination of the mind; buddhimatām, of the intelligent.
commentaries.summary_paraphrase: Shankara reads bījam as the source of growth — the seed-principle from which all beings sprout. The verse extends the essence-rule: Krishna is the discriminating power in those who discriminate, the splendor in those who are splendid. Each named property is named in its pristine form.

commentaries.translator: Swami Adidevananda (Sri Ramakrishna Math edition of Ramanuja's Gita-bhasya, as reproduced on bhagavad-gita.us)
commentaries.verbatim_excerpt: 7.8 – 7.11 All these entities with their peculiar characteristic are born from Me alone. They depend on Me; inasmuch as they constitute My body, they exist in Me alone. Thus I alone exist while all of them are only My modes.
commentaries.summary_paraphrase: Ramanuja's commentary covers 7.8-7.11 as a unit (see 7.8). For 7.10 specifically: seed-of-all-beings, intelligence-of-intelligent, splendor-of-splendid — each named property is Krishna's peculiar mode in that being.

positions.rendering: the original seed of all existences ... the prowess of all powerful men
the eternal seed of all beings ... the splendor of the glorious
the eternal seed-principle, the source of growth, from which all beings sprout
the seed of all beings as Krishna's body-source; intelligence and splendor as his modes

disagreements_among_translators.explanation: Translators agree on the claim. They differ on what kind of seed Krishna is. Shankara's 'source of growth' picks up the agricultural metaphor literally: the seed produces the tree. Ramanuja's reading places the seed in the framework of body-of-God doctrine: every being's existence depends on Krishna as the seed-principle, but the being is Krishna's mode, not Krishna himself. The reading 'splendor/prowess' for tejas varies — Mukundananda's 'splendor' is more aesthetic, Prabhupada's 'prowess' more action-oriented; both are defensible.

literal_meaning: Know me, O Pārtha, as the eternal seed of all beings; I am the intelligence of the intelligent and the splendor of the splendid.
traditional_meaning_consensus: The closing verse of the I-am-the-X-in-Y unit we are generating (the unit continues through 7.11). Krishna names himself as the eternal seed (bījaṁ sanātanam) of all beings — the principle from which beings grow. He then names two more properties: the intelligence (buddhi) of the intelligent, and the splendor (tejas) of the splendid. The verse signals that the I-am-the-X pattern operates at the level of essence/source: Krishna is what makes the property the property, the seed from which the manifest grows. Shankara reads as essence; Ramanuja as mode — both honor the structural claim.
